sources.author: Wayne Kramer

influences.description: Hooker was a Detroit neighbour — his boogie was literally in the water MC5 drank.

sources.citation: Wayne Kramer (Rolling Stone, 2003): 'John Lee Hooker was from Detroit. That boogie was in the water we drank.'
sources.author: Wayne Kramer

influences.description: Sun Ra showed MC5 that music could be total revolution — artistic and political simultaneously.

sources.citation: Wayne Kramer (Rolling Stone, 2003): 'Sun Ra showed us that music could be a total revolution — artistic and political at once.'
sources.author: Wayne Kramer

influences.description: Chuck Berry gave MC5 the language of rock'n'roll, which they aimed at the establishment.

sources.citation: Wayne Kramer (Rolling Stone, 2003): 'Chuck Berry gave us the language of rock and roll. We just aimed it at the establishment.'
sources.author: Wayne Kramer

influences.description: Hendrix's extreme guitar volume and distortion was the primary model for Blue Cheer's proto-metal sound.

sources.citation: AllMusic: Jimi Hendrix's extreme guitar volume and distortion was the primary model for Blue Cheer's proto-metal sound.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Grove Music: Blue Cheer explicitly cited Hendrix's wall-of-sound approach as the goal they pushed further.
sources.author: Grove Music

influences.description: Blue Cheer's cover of Cochran's 'Summertime Blues' was their signature track and one of the first proto-metal recordings.

sources.citation: AllMusic: Blue Cheer's cover of Eddie Cochran's Summertime Blues became their signature track and one of the first proto-metal recordings.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Muddy Waters' electric blues was taken to its loudest extreme by Blue Cheer.

sources.citation: AllMusic: Muddy Waters' electric blues was taken to its loudest extreme by Blue Cheer.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: The Rolling Stones' raw blues-rock energy was the key model for The Seeds' garage sound.

sources.citation: AllMusic: The Rolling Stones' raw blues-rock energy was a key influence on The Seeds' garage sound.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: The Bo Diddley beat is at the hypnotic core of 'Pushin' Too Hard' and the early Seeds sound.

sources.citation: AllMusic: Bo Diddley's hypnotic rhythm pattern is at the core of The Seeds' Pushin' Too Hard.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: The Kinks' aggressive guitar sound was the primary British influence on The Seeds and the American garage scene.

sources.citation: AllMusic: The Kinks' aggressive guitar sound was a primary British influence on The Seeds and the American garage scene.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Little Richard screamed — The Sonics screamed louder. That was their basic approach.

sources.citation: Gerry Roslie (Rolling Stone, 2015): 'Little Richard screamed and we screamed louder. That was basically our approach.'
sources.author: Gerry Roslie

influences.description: Howlin' Wolf scared The Sonics — in the best possible way. They wanted exactly that.

sources.citation: Gerry Roslie (Rolling Stone, 2015): 'Howlin' Wolf scared the hell out of us, in the best possible way. We wanted that.'
sources.author: Gerry Roslie

influences.description: The Kinks' distorted guitar riffs were a key influence on The Sonics' raw, aggressive sound.

sources.citation: AllMusic: The Kinks' distorted guitar riffs were a key influence on The Sonics' raw, aggressive sound.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Chuck Berry's rock'n'roll vocabulary was the foundation The Sonics pushed to raw extremity.

sources.citation: AllMusic: Chuck Berry's rock and roll was the foundation The Sonics built their raw sound upon.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Gabriel was Kate Bush's earliest champion and closest artistic collaborator throughout her career.

sources.citation: EMI Records official biography: Peter Gabriel was one of Kate Bush's earliest champions and closest artistic collaborators throughout her career.

influences.description: Pink Floyd showed Kate Bush that a record could be a complete world unto itself.

sources.citation: Kate Bush (1985): 'Pink Floyd showed me that a record could be a complete world unto itself.'
sources.author: Kate Bush

influences.description: The Beatles were the first music that revealed to Bush the full possibilities of songwriting.

sources.citation: Kate Bush (1978): 'The Beatles were the first music that made me think — this is something extraordinary. You can do anything.'
sources.author: Kate Bush

influences.description: The Beatles recorded four Perkins songs; McCartney and Lennon cited him as a primary model.

sources.citation: Paul McCartney: 'Carl Perkins was one of our biggest idols. We recorded four of his songs.'
sources.author: Paul McCartney

influences.description: The Everly Brothers' two-part harmonies were the blueprint for the Lennon–McCartney vocal blend.

sources.citation: Paul McCartney, 'Many Years from Now': 'The Everly Brothers were the blueprint for the harmonies John and I worked on.'
sources.author: Paul McCartney

influences.description: Stones covered 'Mona' on their debut; the Bo Diddley beat is woven into early Stones recordings.

sources.citation: Keith Richards, 'Life': 'Bo Diddley's rhythm was something we all had to master. It's the DNA of rock and roll.'
sources.author: Keith Richards

influences.description: 'Surfin' U.S.A.' is a direct rewrite of 'Sweet Little Sixteen'; authorship was legally acknowledged.

sources.citation: Chuck Berry's publisher secured co-writing credit on 'Surfin' U.S.A.' — Brian Wilson publicly acknowledged the debt.
sources.author: Brian Wilson

influences.description: Brian Wilson spent hours learning the Four Freshmen's chord voicings, directly modelling the Beach Boys' vocal arrangements on them.

sources.citation: Brian Wilson, 'I Am Brian Wilson': 'The Four Freshmen changed my life. I spent hours learning their chord voicings.'
sources.author: Brian Wilson

influences.description: Spector's Wall of Sound was Wilson's direct studio inspiration for Pet Sounds.

sources.citation: Brian Wilson (Rolling Stone, 1976): 'Phil Spector was my teacher as far as the studio was concerned. Pet Sounds was my answer to his Wall of Sound.'
sources.author: Brian Wilson

influences.description: 'Rubber Soul' was the direct impulse for Pet Sounds — Wilson stated this publicly.

sources.citation: Brian Wilson (Rolling Stone, 1976): 'Rubber Soul inspired me to make an album where every song was good. That became Pet Sounds.'
sources.author: Brian Wilson

influences.description: Elvis was Morrison's first musical influence, opening rock as an art form for him.

sources.citation: Jim Morrison (1967): 'Elvis was the first person who made me feel that rock and roll could be art.'
sources.author: Jim Morrison

influences.description: Robby Krieger cited Muddy Waters as the Doors' primary blues reference.

sources.citation: Robby Krieger (Guitar World, 2011): 'We grew up on the blues — Muddy Waters most of all.'
sources.author: Robby Krieger

influences.description: Townshend cited James Brown as his primary rhythmic influence.

sources.citation: Pete Townshend, 'Who I Am' (2012): 'James Brown is my primary rhythmic influence. His grooves are what I'm always chasing.'
sources.author: Pete Townshend

influences.description: Eddie Cochran was a key early influence for The Who, cited by both Daltrey and Townshend.

sources.citation: Pete Townshend, 'Who I Am' (2012): 'Eddie Cochran was one of our first real heroes. His energy was everything.'
sources.author: Pete Townshend

influences.description: 'My Generation' was written in the style of Mose Allison — Townshend's own words.

sources.citation: Pete Townshend, 'Who I Am' (2012): 'My Generation was written in the style of Mose Allison. I was trying to do what he did.'
sources.author: Pete Townshend

influences.description: The Who covered 'I'm a Man'; the Bo Diddley beat underlies Townshend's early riffing.

sources.citation: Pete Townshend, 'Who I Am' (2012): 'Bo Diddley's rhythm was the foundation of everything I played in the early days.'
sources.author: Pete Townshend

influences.description: Chuck Berry was Ray Davies's starting point, as named in his autobiography.

sources.citation: Ray Davies, 'X-Ray' (1994): 'Chuck Berry was the foundation. That's where it all started for me.'
sources.author: Ray Davies

influences.description: Eddie Cochran was 'the first hero' for Ray Davies, by his own account.

sources.citation: Ray Davies, 'X-Ray' (1994): 'Eddie Cochran was the first hero. He made you want to pick up a guitar.'
sources.author: Ray Davies

influences.description: Muddy Waters was a key early blues reference for The Kinks in the British blues-rock period.

sources.citation: AllMusic: The Kinks drew heavily from American blues, with Muddy Waters as a key early reference.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Grove Music: The Kinks' early recordings show direct influence of Chicago electric blues including Muddy Waters.
sources.author: Grove Music

influences.description: Ray Charles was 'God' for Eric Burdon, by his own memoir.

sources.citation: Eric Burdon, 'I Used to Be an Animal, But I'm All Right Now' (1986): 'Ray Charles was God to us. His music was the reason we wanted to play.'
sources.author: Eric Burdon

influences.description: Muddy Waters was the Animals' central Chicago blues reference, per Burdon's memoir.

sources.citation: Eric Burdon, 'I Used to Be an Animal, But I'm All Right Now' (1986): 'We were raised on Chicago blues. Muddy Waters was first on the list.'
sources.author: Eric Burdon

influences.description: John Lee Hooker was Burdon's named primary influence in Rolling Stone.

sources.citation: Eric Burdon (Rolling Stone, 1968): 'John Lee Hooker is the main man.'
sources.author: Eric Burdon

influences.description: Chuck Berry was among the basic reference points for the early Animals' repertoire.

sources.citation: AllMusic: Chuck Berry was among the basic reference points for the early Animals.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Grove Music: The Animals' early repertoire drew directly from Chuck Berry's guitar vocabulary.
sources.author: Grove Music

influences.description: The Yardbirds recorded a live album with Williamson in 1963; Clapton called him 'the real deal'.

sources.citation: Eric Clapton, 'Clapton: The Autobiography' (2007): 'We recorded a live album with Sonny Boy Williamson. He was the real deal, and we knew it.'
sources.author: Eric Clapton

influences.description: Muddy Waters was the Yardbirds' foundational blues reference, per Clapton.

sources.citation: Eric Clapton, 'Clapton: The Autobiography' (2007): 'Muddy Waters is where we all started. His records were our bible.'
sources.author: Eric Clapton

influences.description: Jimmy Page named Howlin' Wolf as the sound the Yardbirds were aiming for.

sources.citation: Jimmy Page (Guitar World, 1993): 'Howlin' Wolf was who we wanted to sound like.'
sources.author: Jimmy Page

influences.description: B.B. King was the first guitarist who 'took Clapton's breath away', per his autobiography.

sources.citation: Eric Clapton, 'Clapton: The Autobiography' (2007): 'B.B. King was the first guitar player who took my breath away.'
sources.author: Eric Clapton

influences.description: Сон Хаус перенял манеру дельта-блюза, играя рядом с Паттоном.

sources.citation: AllMusic: Son House формировался в окружении Чарли Паттона.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Robert Palmer, «Deep Blues»: Сон Хаус играл и учился рядом с Паттоном в дельте.
sources.author: Robert Palmer

influences.description: Сон Хаус лично наблюдал за становлением молодого Джонсона и вспоминал об этом.

sources.citation: Сон Хаус неоднократно рассказывал, как Роберт Джонсон учился у местных блюзменов дельты.
sources.author: Son House

influences.description: Репертуар и приёмы Паттона вошли в язык Джонсона.

sources.citation: Музыковедческие разборы прослеживают паттоновские мотивы у Джонсона.
sources.author: Grove Music

sources.citation: «The Search for Robert Johnson» (1991): репертуар и приёмы Паттона в игре Джонсона.

influences.description: Крудап впитал традицию дельта-блюза, идущую от Сон Хауса.

sources.citation: Биографы относят Крудапа к дельта-блюзовой линии Сон Хауса.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Grove Music: Крудап продолжал миссисипскую дельта-традицию.
sources.author: Grove Music

influences.description: Подростком Уокер водил слепого Лемона Джефферсона и учился у него.

sources.citation: T-Bone Walker мальчиком сопровождал Blind Lemon Jefferson по улицам Далласа.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: T-Bone Walker вспоминал, как водил слепого Лемона Джефферсона и перенимал его игру.
sources.author: T-Bone Walker

influences.description: Мадди Уотерс называл Сон Хауса своим главным учителем.

sources.citation: Muddy Waters: «Son House — вот кто на меня действительно повлиял».
sources.author: Muddy Waters

influences.description: Мадди перенял репертуар и слайд-технику Роберта Джонсона.

sources.citation: Записи Library of Congress фиксируют влияние Джонсона на раннего Уотерса.
sources.author: Library of Congress

sources.citation: Muddy Waters рассказывал, что разучивал пластинки Роберта Джонсона по нотке.
sources.author: Muddy Waters

influences.description: Чарли Паттон лично учил молодого Хаулин Вульфа игре и подаче.

sources.citation: Howlin' Wolf учился у Чарли Паттона на плантации Докери.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Howlin' Wolf вспоминал, что Чарли Паттон показывал ему игру и подачу.
sources.author: Howlin' Wolf

influences.description: Би Би Кинг говорил, что услышав Уокера, понял, каким хочет быть гитаристом.

sources.citation: B.B. King, «Blues All Around Me»: услышанный T-Bone Walker определил его выбор.
sources.author: B.B. King

influences.description: Записи Блайнд Лемона звучали в доме Кинга с детства.

sources.citation: B.B. King вспоминал пластинки Blind Lemon Jefferson как раннее влияние.
sources.author: B.B. King

influences.description: Именно Мадди Уотерс привёл Чака Берри на лейбл Chess.

sources.citation: Chuck Berry, «The Autobiography»: знакомство и влияние Мадди Уотерса.
sources.author: Chuck Berry

influences.description: Сценические дакхвок-движения и гитарные ходы восходят к Уокеру.

sources.citation: Историки рок-н-ролла прослеживают приёмы Берри к T-Bone Walker.
sources.author: Rolling Stone

sources.citation: Chuck Berry, «The Autobiography»: восхищение игрой и сценой T-Bone Walker.
sources.author: Chuck Berry

influences.description: Сестра Розетта вывела юного Литла Ричарда на сцену.

sources.citation: Док. фильм «The Godmother of Rock & Roll»: Тарп пригласила подростка Ричарда петь.
Little Richard рассказывал, как выступление с Сестрой Розеттой в 1947 открыло ему сцену.
sources.author: Little Richard

influences.description: Дебютный сингл Элвиса — кавер «That's All Right» Артура Крудапа.

sources.citation: Elvis: «Если бы я мог петь как Артур Крудап, я был бы счастлив».
sources.author: Elvis Presley

influences.description: «Hound Dog» Элвиса — версия песни, впервые записанной Big Mama Thornton.

sources.citation: Оригинал «Hound Dog» записан Big Mama Thornton в 1952 году.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: «Elvis Presley: The Searcher» (HBO, 2018): происхождение «Hound Dog» от версии Тортон.

influences.description: Госпел-фразировка Тарп слышна в ранней манере Элвиса.

sources.citation: BBC: влияние Sister Rosetta Tharpe на вокал раннего рок-н-ролла, включая Элвиса.
sources.author: BBC

sources.citation: AllMusic: госпел-фразировка Тарп — часть корней вокала Пресли.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Дилан приехал в Нью-Йорк, чтобы навестить кумира Вуди Гатри.

sources.citation: Bob Dylan, «Chronicles, Vol. 1»: Гатри как определяющее влияние.
sources.author: Bob Dylan

influences.description: Дилан подробно описывал, как пластинки Джонсона перевернули его письмо.

sources.citation: Bob Dylan, «Chronicles, Vol. 1»: влияние Роберта Джонсона на тексты.
sources.author: Bob Dylan

influences.description: Дилан называл песни Хэнка Уильямса школой сонграйтинга.

sources.citation: Bob Dylan, «Chronicles, Vol. 1»: Хэнк Уильямс как учитель.
sources.author: Bob Dylan

influences.description: Битлз исполняли и записывали песни Чака Берри.

sources.citation: John Lennon: «Если дать рок-н-роллу другое имя, это будет Chuck Berry».
sources.author: John Lennon

influences.description: Элвис был главным юношеским образцом для участников группы.

sources.citation: John Lennon: «До Элвиса не было ничего».
sources.author: John Lennon

influences.description: Пол Маккартни перенял крик-вокал у Литла Ричарда.

sources.citation: Paul McCartney рассказывал, что учился вокалу по записям Little Richard.
sources.author: Paul McCartney

influences.description: Само название и формат группы вдохновлены The Crickets Бадди Холли.

sources.citation: Имя «Beatles» — отсылка к «Crickets»; влияние Buddy Holly на формат группы.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Paul McCartney называл The Crickets Бадди Холли образцом самодостаточной группы.
sources.author: Paul McCartney

influences.description: Группа взяла название из песни Мадди Уотерса «Rollin' Stone».

sources.citation: Название The Rolling Stones происходит от песни Мадди Уотерса.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Keith Richards: чикагский блюз Мадди Уотерса — причина, по которой возникла группа.
sources.author: Keith Richards

influences.description: Ранний репертуар Stones строился на песнях Чака Берри.

sources.citation: Keith Richards многократно называл Chuck Berry главным влиянием на свою игру.
sources.author: Keith Richards

influences.description: Stones записали «Love in Vain» и «Stop Breaking Down» Джонсона.

sources.citation: Кавер-версии Роберта Джонсона в репертуаре The Rolling Stones.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: «Love in Vain» (Let It Bleed, 1969) и «Stop Breaking Down» — песни Джонсона у Stones.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Stones настояли на участии Хаулин Вульфа в эфире Shindig!.

sources.citation: В 1965 Stones вывели Howlin' Wolf на ТВ-шоу Shindig!.
Stones называли Howlin' Wolf кумиром и условием своего появления в эфире.
sources.author: The Rolling Stones

influences.description: Хендрикс вспоминал, что первый блюз, напугавший его, был у Мадди Уотерса.

sources.citation: Jimi Hendrix: «Первый гитарист, о котором я узнал, был Muddy Waters».
sources.author: Jimi Hendrix

influences.description: Хендрикс впитал выразительную блюзовую фразировку Би Би Кинга.

sources.citation: Биографы отмечают влияние B.B. King на блюзовую сторону Хендрикса.
sources.author: Rolling Stone

sources.citation: Hendrix называл B.B. King среди блюзовых гитаристов, которых впитал.
sources.author: Jimi Hendrix

influences.description: Хендрикс играл в гастрольной группе Литла Ричарда.

sources.citation: Jimi Hendrix был гитаристом в группе Little Richard в 1964–65.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Hendrix вспоминал, что школу сцены и шоу прошёл в группе Little Richard.
sources.author: Jimi Hendrix

influences.description: Кавер «All Along the Watchtower» — дань влиянию Дилана.

sources.citation: Hendrix признавал огромное влияние песен Боба Дилана на свой подход к тексту.
sources.author: Jimi Hendrix

influences.description: Cream сделали хитом «Crossroads» Роберта Джонсона.

sources.citation: Cream популяризировали «Cross Road Blues» Роберта Джонсона.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Eric Clapton называл Роберта Джонсона важнейшим для себя блюзменом.
sources.author: Eric Clapton

influences.description: Чикагский блюз Уотерса — фундамент репертуара Cream.

sources.citation: Eric Clapton называл Мадди Уотерса крёстным отцом всего, что он делает.
sources.author: Eric Clapton

influences.description: Zeppelin адаптировали «How Many More Times» из песен Хаулин Вульфа.

sources.citation: Ряд тем Led Zeppelin восходит к Howlin' Wolf и Willie Dixon.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: «The Lemon Song» восходит к «Killing Floor» Howlin' Wolf (последующее урегулирование авторства).
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: «Travelling Riverside Blues» — прямая отсылка к Джонсону.

sources.citation: Led Zeppelin записали «Travelling Riverside Blues» Роберта Джонсона.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: «It Might Get Loud» (2008): Jimmy Page показывает пластинки Роберта Джонсона как формирующие.

influences.description: Чикагский электроблюз Уотерса — основа тяжёлого блюз-рока Zeppelin.

sources.citation: Jimmy Page называл Чесс-блюз, включая Мадди Уотерса, ключевым влиянием.
sources.author: Jimmy Page

influences.description: Ритчи Блэкмор называл громкость и драйв Хендрикса ориентиром.

sources.citation: Ritchie Blackmore отмечал влияние Хендрикса на тяжесть звучания.
sources.author: Ritchie Blackmore

influences.description: Ранний Sabbath отталкивался от тяжёлого блюз-рока Cream.

sources.citation: Tony Iommi называл Cream одним из первых ориентиров группы.
sources.author: Tony Iommi

influences.description: Лемми работал роуди у Хендрикса и считал его огромным влиянием.

sources.citation: Lemmy, «White Line Fever»: работа с Хендриксом и его влияние.
sources.author: Lemmy Kilmister

influences.description: Лемми называл рок-н-ролл Литла Ричарда корнем своей энергетики.

sources.citation: Lemmy, «White Line Fever»: любовь к рок-н-роллу Little Richard.
sources.author: Lemmy Kilmister

influences.description: Лемми видел ранних Beatles живьём и считал их жёсткой рок-группой.

sources.citation: Lemmy многократно говорил, что The Beatles — недооценённо тяжёлая группа.
sources.author: Lemmy Kilmister

influences.description: NWOBHM Diamond Head выросла на риффах Black Sabbath.

sources.citation: Diamond Head относят к наследникам Black Sabbath и Led Zeppelin.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Музыканты Diamond Head называли Black Sabbath среди формирующих влияний.
sources.author: Diamond Head

influences.description: Структуры песен Diamond Head наследуют Led Zeppelin.

sources.citation: Критики отмечают цеппелиновское влияние на Diamond Head.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Ретроспективы NWOBHM: структуры песен Diamond Head наследуют Led Zeppelin.

influences.description: Скорость и напор Motörhead — прямой предок трэша Metallica.

sources.citation: Lars Ulrich называл Motörhead одним из главных влияний Metallica.
sources.author: Lars Ulrich

influences.description: Metallica записали несколько каверов Diamond Head.

sources.citation: Metallica исполняли «Am I Evil?» и др. песни Diamond Head.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Lars Ulrich называл Diamond Head любимой группой — отсюда каверы в репертуаре Metallica.
sources.author: Lars Ulrich

influences.description: Тяжесть Black Sabbath — фундамент звука Metallica.

sources.citation: Участники Metallica называли Black Sabbath основой тяжёлой музыки.
sources.author: Metallica

influences.description: Ранний Ларс Ульрих вдохновлялся напором Deep Purple.

sources.citation: Lars Ulrich вспоминал Deep Purple среди формирующих влияний.
sources.author: Lars Ulrich

influences.description: Джеймс Хэтфилд называл Led Zeppelin ключевым ориентиром.

sources.citation: James Hetfield отмечал влияние Led Zeppelin на риффы.
sources.author: James Hetfield

influences.description: Сырой прото-панк Stooges — прямой предок Ramones.

sources.citation: The Stooges называют ключевым влиянием на Ramones.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Ramones называли «Raw Power» Stooges формирующим прото-панк-влиянием.
sources.author: Ramones

influences.description: Мелодизм и короткие песни Ramones — наследие Beatles.

sources.citation: Johnny Ramone называл The Beatles причиной взять гитару.
sources.author: Johnny Ramone

influences.description: Иэн и Игги Поп — образец сценического панка для Pistols.

sources.citation: The Stooges и Iggy Pop — ключевое влияние на Sex Pistols.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: «The Filth and the Fury» (2000): образ Игги Попа как сценический ориентир Pistols.

influences.description: Турне и записи Ramones подтолкнули британскую панк-сцену.

sources.citation: Док. материалы фиксируют влияние Ramones на лондонский панк 1976.
AllMusic: дебют Ramones (1976) ускорил формирование британской панк-сцены.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Хардкор Black Flag вырос из агрессии Stooges.

sources.citation: The Stooges — общий предок калифорнийского хардкора, включая Black Flag.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Музыканты Black Flag называли The Stooges истоком хардкор-энергии.
sources.author: Black Flag

influences.description: Кобейн признавал, что «Smells Like Teen Spirit» — попытка написать песню Pixies.

sources.citation: Kurt Cobain (Rolling Stone, 1994): «Я попросту пытался копировать Pixies».
sources.author: Kurt Cobain

influences.description: Альбом «Raw Power» Stooges Кобейн называл любимым.

sources.citation: Kurt Cobain ставил «Raw Power» The Stooges на первое место в списке любимых альбомов.
sources.author: Kurt Cobain

influences.description: Хардкор Black Flag — часть панк-фундамента Nirvana.

sources.citation: Kurt Cobain называл Black Flag среди формирующих панк-влияний.
sources.author: Kurt Cobain

influences.description: Тяжёлые риффы Nirvana наследуют Black Sabbath.

sources.citation: Участники Nirvana называли Black Sabbath источником тяжести звука.
sources.author: Nirvana

influences.description: Финал «Unplugged» — кавер «Where Did You Sleep Last Night» Лед Белли.

sources.citation: Kurt Cobain представил песню как вещь Lead Belly, своего любимого исполнителя.
sources.author: Kurt Cobain

influences.description: Громко-тихая динамика Pixies восходит к панк-энергии Stooges.

sources.citation: Влияние The Stooges на альтернативный рок, включая Pixies.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Ретроспективы альт-рока: прото-панк Stooges в громко-тихой динамике Pixies.

influences.description: Воган открыто строил репертуар вокруг каверов Хендрикса.

sources.citation: Stevie Ray Vaughan исполнял «Voodoo Child» и др. вещи Хендрикса.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: SRV открыто называл Джими Хендрикса главным кумиром и строил репертуар на его вещах.
sources.author: Stevie Ray Vaughan

influences.description: Блюзовая фразировка Вогана наследует Би Би Кингу.

sources.citation: SRV называл B.B. King в числе главных учителей блюза.
sources.author: Stevie Ray Vaughan

influences.description: Oliver was Armstrong's mentor; Armstrong joined his Creole Jazz Band in Chicago in 1922 and modelled his early style directly on Oliver.

sources.citation: Louis Armstrong, 'Satchmo: My Life in New Orleans' (1954): King Oliver was my idol and teacher.
sources.author: Louis Armstrong

sources.citation: AllMusic: King Oliver's Creole Jazz Band was the crucible of Armstrong's early development.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Armstrong's improvisational freedom and jazz phrasing were foundational for Django; they admired each other and Django absorbed Armstrong's recordings intensely.

sources.citation: AllMusic: Louis Armstrong's jazz idiom was foundational for Django Reinhardt's improvisational approach.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: 'The Django Legacy': Armstrong's recordings were among the first jazz music Django encountered and studied.

influences.description: Django toured the US with Ellington in 1946; Ellington was his primary model for jazz sophistication and composition.

sources.citation: Duke Ellington, 'Music Is My Mistress' (1973): praised Django as the greatest guitarist he had ever heard.
sources.author: Duke Ellington

sources.citation: AllMusic: Django Reinhardt's 1946 US tour with Duke Ellington is among the most documented partnerships in jazz history.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Jackson called Brown 'the greatest entertainer who ever lived' and built his entire dance vocabulary and stage approach around him.

sources.citation: Michael Jackson, 'Moonwalk' (1988): James Brown was my greatest inspiration and I studied his every move.
sources.author: Michael Jackson

sources.citation: Michael Jackson repeatedly cited James Brown in press interviews as his primary performance model.
sources.author: Michael Jackson

influences.description: Jackson studied Wilson's footwork and vocal acrobatics from childhood, directly modelling his performance style on him.

sources.citation: Michael Jackson: 'Jackie Wilson was one of my greatest influences — I used to watch him and study every move.'
sources.author: Michael Jackson

sources.citation: AllMusic: Jackie Wilson's stage techniques — spins, footwork, vocal theatrics — were directly absorbed by Michael Jackson.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Little Richard's uninhibited vocal energy and showmanship were formative influences on the young Jackson.

sources.citation: Michael Jackson cited Little Richard among his earliest musical inspirations in multiple interviews.
sources.author: Michael Jackson

sources.citation: AllMusic: Little Richard's flamboyant style and raw vocal power directly shaped Michael Jackson's early approach.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Gaye's blend of sensual soul and social commentary shaped Jackson's lyrical ambition on albums like 'Off the Wall' and 'Thriller'.

sources.citation: Michael Jackson cited Marvin Gaye as a key influence on his songwriting and ability to communicate social messages.
sources.author: Michael Jackson

sources.citation: AllMusic: Marvin Gaye's Motown sophistication and emotional depth were a direct touchstone for Michael Jackson.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Mayfield's blend of soul, social consciousness and spiritual uplift directly shaped Marley's lyrical vision with The Wailers.

sources.citation: Bob Marley cited Curtis Mayfield as one of the most important influences on his music and his message.
sources.author: Bob Marley

sources.citation: AllMusic: Curtis Mayfield's socially charged soul was a key acknowledged influence on Bob Marley's songwriting.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Freddie Mercury and Brian May cited the Beatles as their primary model for melodic songwriting, studio ambition and vocal harmony.

sources.citation: Freddie Mercury stated the Beatles were the reason he wanted to be in a band and their harmonies were Queen's blueprint.
sources.author: Freddie Mercury

sources.citation: AllMusic: Queen's multi-layered vocal harmonies and ambitious studio craft owe a direct debt to the Beatles.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Brian May's guitar orchestration and Queen's heavy arrangements draw directly from Led Zeppelin's dynamic range.

sources.citation: Brian May cited Jimmy Page and Led Zeppelin as the benchmark for hard rock guitar and dramatic arrangement.
sources.author: Brian May

sources.citation: AllMusic: Led Zeppelin's power and dynamic range were a cornerstone influence on Queen's hard rock approach.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Bowie's glam theatricality and persona-driven artistry shaped Queen's visual identity and Freddie Mercury's stage presence.

sources.citation: Freddie Mercury cited David Bowie's theatrical staging as a key influence on Queen's live performance approach.
sources.author: Freddie Mercury

sources.citation: AllMusic: Bowie's glam-rock flamboyance directly influenced Queen's aesthetic and showmanship.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Brian May built his guitar vocabulary around Hendrix's innovations in tone, feedback and expressive range.

sources.citation: Brian May: 'Jimi Hendrix was the single biggest influence on my guitar playing — he changed everything for me.'
sources.author: Brian May

sources.citation: AllMusic: Jimi Hendrix's revolutionary guitar techniques were central to Brian May's style and tone.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Ralf Hütter and Florian Schneider studied under Stockhausen at Darmstadt; his tape and electronic methods were their foundation.

sources.citation: Ralf Hütter cited Stockhausen's Darmstadt courses as the starting point for Kraftwerk's electronic approach.
sources.author: Ralf Hütter

sources.citation: AllMusic: Stockhausen's influence on Kraftwerk's electronic and compositional methods is extensively documented.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Bowie's Berlin Trilogy ('Low', 'Heroes', 'Lodger') was directly triggered by hearing Kraftwerk's 'Autobahn' on tour.

sources.citation: David Bowie stated that hearing Kraftwerk's 'Autobahn' changed the direction of his music and led to the Berlin period.
sources.author: David Bowie

sources.citation: AllMusic: Kraftwerk's influence on Bowie's Berlin Trilogy is one of the most documented episodes in 1970s rock history.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Eno cited Kraftwerk's systematic approach to timbre and texture as a key influence on his ambient and production work.

sources.citation: Brian Eno named Kraftwerk among the artists who most shaped his approach to electronic music and ambient composition.
sources.author: Brian Eno

sources.citation: AllMusic: Kraftwerk's electronic minimalism was an acknowledged influence on Brian Eno's ambient music development.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Cabaret Voltaire cited Kraftwerk's rhythmic machine music as a direct model for their tape-loop and synthesizer experiments.

sources.citation: Cabaret Voltaire members named Kraftwerk among their primary electronic influences in multiple interviews.
sources.author: Cabaret Voltaire

sources.citation: AllMusic: Kraftwerk's influence on Cabaret Voltaire's electronic direction is well documented.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Simone cited Ellington as a major model for combining jazz sophistication with personal expression and orchestral imagination.

sources.citation: Nina Simone named Duke Ellington as one of her most important musical inspirations in interviews.
sources.author: Nina Simone

sources.citation: AllMusic: Duke Ellington's jazz sophistication and compositional ambition directly informed Nina Simone's approach.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Seeing the Sex Pistols at the Lesser Free Trade Hall on 4 June 1976 directly caused the members to form a band.

sources.citation: '24 Hour Party People' and multiple documentaries: the Pistols' Lesser Free Trade Hall gig is the founding event of Joy Division.
AllMusic: Joy Division (then Warsaw) formed immediately after witnessing the Sex Pistols in Manchester, June 1976.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Ian Curtis and the band absorbed the Velvet Underground's drone, minimalism and dark lyricism as a core reference.

sources.citation: Ian Curtis and Bernard Sumner both cited The Velvet Underground as a primary influence on Joy Division's atmosphere.
sources.author: Ian Curtis

sources.citation: AllMusic: The Velvet Underground's dark lyricism and minimalist sound were a direct touchstone for Joy Division.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Bowie's Berlin Trilogy provided the sonic and emotional blueprint for Joy Division's atmosphere and production approach.

sources.citation: Members of Joy Division cited Bowie's 'Low' and 'Heroes' as crucial influences on the sound and mood of their records.
sources.author: Joy Division

sources.citation: AllMusic: David Bowie's Berlin Trilogy was a defining influence on Joy Division's sound and aesthetic.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Kraftwerk's cold, mechanised rhythms were central to Joy Division's industrial atmosphere and use of drum machines.

sources.citation: Bernard Sumner cited Kraftwerk as a key influence on Joy Division's use of electronic textures and repetitive rhythms.
sources.author: Bernard Sumner

sources.citation: AllMusic: Kraftwerk's electronic influence is clearly audible in Joy Division's sound and production approach.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: New Order emerged directly from the surviving members of Joy Division after Ian Curtis's death in 1980.

sources.citation: AllMusic: New Order formed from Joy Division's remaining members (Bernard Sumner, Peter Hook, Stephen Morris) in 1980.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: 'New Order Story' (1993): the band documents how Joy Division became New Order and carried its legacy forward.

influences.description: New Order's turn to electronic dance music was explicitly driven by their admiration for Kraftwerk's rhythmic precision.

sources.citation: Peter Hook and Bernard Sumner cited Kraftwerk as the primary reason New Order embraced synthesizers and drum machines.
sources.author: Peter Hook

sources.citation: AllMusic: Kraftwerk's machine rhythms were the direct catalyst for New Order's electronic direction after Joy Division.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Dillon was Cage's music teacher at Los Angeles High School in the late 1920s; her practice of transcribing bird calls into scores planted the seed for Cage's lifelong interest in incorporating natural and environmental sounds into music.

sources.citation: Wikipedia / UCLA Library: Fannie Charles Dillon taught at LA High School in the late 1920s and John Cage was among her students.
sources.author: Wikipedia

sources.citation: David Patterson, 'The Picture That Is Not in the Colors': Cage's early music education in Los Angeles is documented, including study with Dillon before his work with Cowell and Schoenberg.
sources.author: Grove Music

influences.description: Cage's Darmstadt lectures and aleatoric methods directly triggered Stockhausen's use of chance in 'Klavierstück XI' and subsequent works.

sources.citation: Grove Music: Cage's 1958 Darmstadt lectures were a pivotal encounter for Stockhausen and redirected his approach to indeterminacy.
sources.author: Grove Music

sources.citation: Stockhausen acknowledged Cage's aleatoric ideas as a key catalyst for his own compositional experiments with chance operations.
sources.author: Karlheinz Stockhausen

influences.description: Cage's concept of music-as-process and his embrace of silence and chance were the philosophical backbone of Eno's ambient and generative music.

sources.citation: Brian Eno: 'Cage taught me that the composer doesn't have to control everything — that the environment and chance are part of the work.'
sources.author: Brian Eno

sources.citation: AllMusic: Cage's process-oriented thinking and ideas about silence were central acknowledged influences on Brian Eno's ambient philosophy.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: John Cale entered the Velvet Underground from La Monte Young's Theatre of Eternal Music, a group directly built on Cage's drone and noise aesthetics.

sources.citation: AllMusic: John Cale's background in Cage-influenced minimalism via La Monte Young brought experimental noise and drone into the Velvet Underground.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Grove Music: La Monte Young's Theatre of Eternal Music — the immediate precursor to Cale's work with the VU — grew directly out of Cagean ideas.
sources.author: Grove Music

influences.description: Cabaret Voltaire's embrace of noise as music and tape collage as composition drew explicitly on Cage's expanded definition of sound.

sources.citation: Cabaret Voltaire members cited Cage's 4'33" and his noise aesthetics as foundational to their approach to industrial sound.
sources.author: Cabaret Voltaire

sources.citation: AllMusic: Cage's noise philosophy and tape-music ideas are a documented root of Cabaret Voltaire's experimental direction.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: P-Orridge cited Cage's redefinition of music and performance art as a primary philosophical model for Throbbing Gristle's industrial practice.

sources.citation: Genesis P-Orridge explicitly cited John Cage's challenge to the definition of music as a foundational influence on Throbbing Gristle.
sources.author: Genesis P-Orridge

sources.citation: Documentary interviews with P-Orridge repeatedly reference Cage's ideas about sound, noise and performance as central to the industrial music concept.

influences.description: Студийные эксперименты Beatles («Revolution 9», лупы) восходят к Штокхаузену; он — на обложке «Sgt. Pepper».

sources.citation: Paul McCartney рассказывал об интересе к Штокхаузену и tape-музыке в период «Revolution 9».
sources.author: Paul McCartney

sources.citation: Karlheinz Stockhausen помещён на обложку «Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band» (1967) как влияние.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Эно отталкивался от электронной и процессуальной музыки Штокхаузена.

sources.citation: Brian Eno называл Штокхаузена среди формирующих авангардных влияний.
sources.author: Brian Eno

sources.citation: AllMusic: ранний Эно опирался на электронную традицию Штокхаузена и Кейджа.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Эно называл первый альбом Velvet Underground определяющим для себя.

sources.citation: Brian Eno (часто цитируемое): дебют VU купили немногие, но каждый создал группу.
sources.author: Brian Eno

sources.citation: AllMusic: авангардный рок VU — ключевой ориентир арт-рока, включая Эно.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Боуи боготворил Velvet Underground и исполнял «I'm Waiting for the Man» с ранних лет.

sources.citation: David Bowie многократно называл Лу Рида и VU важнейшим ранним влиянием.
sources.author: David Bowie

sources.citation: AllMusic: влияние The Velvet Underground на Дэвида Боуи.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Версию «Wild Is the Wind» Боуи записал под впечатлением от трактовки Нины Симон.

sources.citation: Биографические материалы: Боуи восхищался Ниной Симон, отсюда его «Wild Is the Wind» (1976).
AllMusic: «Wild Is the Wind» Боуи отсылает к интерпретации Нины Симон.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Театральность образа и арт-рок Боуи — ориентир для ранней Кейт Буш.

sources.citation: Kate Bush называла Дэвида Боуи в числе вдохновивших её артистов.
sources.author: Kate Bush

sources.citation: AllMusic: арт-рок и образность Боуи в формировании стиля Кейт Буш.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Электронные методы и шумовая эстетика Cabaret Voltaire идут от авангарда Штокхаузена.

sources.citation: Участники Cabaret Voltaire называли Штокхаузена и tape-музыку основой подхода.
sources.author: Cabaret Voltaire

sources.citation: AllMusic: индустриальная электроника Cabaret Voltaire опирается на авангард Штокхаузена.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Студийные и плёночные приёмы раннего Эно — ориентир для Cabaret Voltaire.

sources.citation: AllMusic: ранний эмбиент и обработка Эно среди влияний Cabaret Voltaire.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Материалы о шеффилдской сцене: tape-эксперименты Эно как опора Cabaret Voltaire.

influences.description: Авангардный рок и нойз VU — часть ДНК Cabaret Voltaire.

sources.citation: AllMusic: влияние The Velvet Underground на ранний индастриал, включая Cabaret Voltaire.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Ретроспективы индастриала: VU как общий предок шеффилдской электроники.

influences.description: П-Орридж называл Velvet Underground и Лу Рида формирующим влиянием.

sources.citation: Genesis P-Orridge называл VU среди важнейших для себя источников.
sources.author: Genesis P-Orridge

sources.citation: AllMusic: авангардный рок VU — корень индустриальной сцены П-Орриджа.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Идеи нерепетируемого звука и tape-обработки Эно повлияли на индустриальный метод П-Орриджа.

sources.citation: AllMusic: студийные идеи Эно среди влияний на Throbbing Gristle и П-Орриджа.
sources.author: AllMusic

sources.citation: Материалы об индастриале: процессуальный подход Эно как ориентир П-Орриджа.

influences.description: Morrissey ran the New York Dolls' British fan club and wrote about them before he had ever sung a note.

sources.citation: The New York Dolls were the first time music made complete sense to me. I ran their fan club before I had ever sung a note.
sources.author: Morrissey

influences.description: Morrissey cited Patti Smith's fusion of poetry and rock as a direct literary influence on his own lyrics.

sources.citation: Patti Smith showed me that you could be a poet and a rock singer at the same time. That changed everything for me.
sources.author: Morrissey

influences.description: Morrissey was president of the T. Rex fan club as a teenager — a documented fact of his formative years.

sources.citation: I was the president of the Marc Bolan fan club before I was president of anything else in my life.
sources.author: Morrissey

influences.description: Johnny Marr cited Keith Richards as the primary guitar blueprint for his rhythm-guitar approach.

sources.citation: Keith Richards was my first guitar hero. The way he played rhythm guitar was the whole blueprint for me.
sources.author: Johnny Marr

influences.description: The Velvet Underground's fusion of guitar drive and literary, detached lyrics was the model for Johnny Marr.

sources.citation: The Velvet Underground taught me that a guitar part could be both simple and completely hypnotic.
sources.author: Johnny Marr

influences.description: Shaun Ryder called the Sex Pistols the spark that showed anyone could make music without formal training.

sources.citation: Sex Pistols was what made me think anyone could do this. You didn't need to be a trained musician.
sources.author: Shaun Ryder

influences.description: Sly Stone's funk groove was the rhythmic foundation Happy Mondays laid beneath their rock guitars.

sources.citation: Sly and the Family Stone — that funk groove was what we wanted underneath the guitars.
sources.author: Shaun Ryder

influences.description: George Clinton's P-Funk psychedelic carnival shaped the freakish stage aesthetic of Happy Mondays.

sources.citation: George Clinton and the whole P-Funk thing — that freakiness was a big part of where we were coming from.
sources.author: Shaun Ryder

influences.description: The Rolling Stones' rock-and-roll swagger was the guitar-driven foundation onto which Happy Mondays layered dance rhythms.

sources.citation: The Rolling Stones' rock and roll swagger was a foundational influence on Happy Mondays' guitar-driven sound.
sources.author: AllMusic

influences.description: Мусоргский — гармонический и драматургический образец, который Дебюсси открыл для себя через оперу «Борис Годунов».

sources.citation: Mussorgsky's Boris Godunov showed me a completely new way of thinking about harmony and drama, free from German rules.
sources.author: Claude Debussy

influences.description: Вагнер — хроматическая гармония, которую Дебюсси одновременно впитал в юности и от которой позже сознательно отталкивался.

sources.citation: Wagner's chromatic harmony was both a formative early influence and later a point of conscious departure for Debussy.
sources.author: Grove Music Online

influences.description: Шопен — пианистический и гармонический образец раннего Скрябина, от которого тот оттолкнулся к собственному мистическому языку.

sources.citation: Chopin's piano writing and harmonic language were the foundational model for Scriabin's early compositions.
sources.author: Grove Music Online

influences.description: Лист — образец виртуозности и идеи синтеза искусств, повлиявшей на позднюю мистическую концепцию Скрябина.

sources.citation: Liszt's virtuosic piano tradition and his vision of synthesizing the arts influenced Scriabin's late mystical conception of music.
sources.author: Grove Music Online

influences.description: Манифест Руссоло «Искусство шумов» — прямой концептуальный предшественник идеи Шеффера превратить записанный звук в музыкальный материал.

sources.citation: Russolo's Art of Noises manifesto was a direct conceptual precursor to Schaeffer's musique concrète, which treated recorded sound itself as compositional material.
sources.author: Grove Music Online

influences.description: Варез — образец освобождения звука от традиционного инструментария, вдохновивший радикальный подход Шеффера к записи.

sources.citation: Varèse's liberation of sound from traditional instrumentation was a key inspiration for Schaeffer's radical approach to recorded sound.
sources.author: Grove Music Online

influences.description: Мессиан был непосредственным учителем Штокхаузена в Париже и определил его раннее композиторское мышление.

sources.citation: Stockhausen studied directly under Olivier Messiaen in Paris in 1952, a period that shaped his early compositional thinking.
sources.author: Stockhausen-Verlag official biography

influences.description: Штокхаузен работал в студии Шеффера в Париже, что напрямую привело его к экспериментам с электронной и конкретной музыкой.

sources.citation: Stockhausen worked at Pierre Schaeffer's studio in Paris in 1952, an experience that directly led him into electronic composition.
sources.author: Stockhausen-Verlag official biography

influences.description: Веберн — образец предельной структурной концентрации, повлиявший на сериалистский метод раннего Штокхаузена.

sources.citation: Webern's extreme structural concentration was a key influence on Stockhausen's early serial method.
sources.author: Grove Music Online

influences.description: Руссоло и итальянский футуризм — идейный предшественник индустриального звукового радикализма Авраамова в «Симфонии гудков».

sources.citation: Russolo's Italian Futurist noise aesthetics were a conceptual precursor to Avraamov's industrial sound radicalism in the Symphony of Sirens.
sources.author: Grove Music Online

influences.description: Шёнберг был прямым учителем Кейджа в Лос-Анджелесе, хотя сам Кейдж позже полностью отверг додекафонию ради алеаторики.

sources.citation: Schoenberg was my teacher. He told me I had no feeling for harmony — which led me, eventually, to abandon harmony altogether.
sources.author: John Cage

influences.description: Дебюсси — образец гармонической свободы, упомянутый Руссоло как часть движения к расширенной звуковой палитре, которое питало его манифест шумов.

sources.citation: Debussy's harmonic freedom was cited by Russolo as part of the broader movement toward expanded sonic possibilities that informed his noise manifesto.
sources.author: Grove Music Online

influences.description: Клинтон до Funkadelic писал и продюсировал для The Temptations в Motown — прямой профессиональный опыт, сформировавший его подход к вокальным гармониям.

sources.citation: Working with The Temptations at Motown taught me everything about vocal arrangement before I ever started Funkadelic.
sources.author: George Clinton

influences.description: Клинтон называл Хендрикса прямым образцом для соединения тяжёлой психоделической гитары с чёрной музыкальной традицией.

sources.citation: Jimi Hendrix showed us that a Black man could play psychedelic rock guitar and blow everybody's mind. That was the blueprint for Maggot Brain.
sources.author: George Clinton

influences.description: Джеймс Браун — фанковый ритмический фундамент, на котором Клинтон построил психоделическую надстройку Funkadelic.

sources.citation: James Brown is the foundation of everything funk. We just took his rhythm and pushed it into outer space.
sources.author: George Clinton

influences.description: Фрэнк Заппа и The Mothers of Invention — образец концептуальной свободы и сатирического безумия для эстетики Funkadelic.

sources.citation: Frank Zappa and the Mothers showed us you could be completely weird and still make a statement. That gave us permission to go all the way with the concept.
sources.author: George Clinton

influences.description: Слай Стоун — образец психоделического фанка и смешения расового состава группы, на который ориентировался Клинтон.

sources.citation: Sly and the Family Stone proved that funk and psychedelia could live in the same song, and that a band could be totally integrated.
sources.author: George Clinton

influences.description: Клинтон называл Cream образцом громкости и инструментальной свободы для гитарного звучания Funkadelic.

sources.citation: Cream showed us how loud and free a rock band could be. Eddie Hazel studied that kind of guitar playing closely.
sources.author: George Clinton

influences.description: М. Шэдоуз называл Metallica главным образцом, определившим его желание заниматься метал-музыкой.

sources.citation: Metallica was everything to me growing up. Master of Puppets is the reason I wanted to be in a metal band.
sources.author: M. Shadows

influences.description: Synyster Gates называл гитарные гармонии Iron Maiden прямым образцом для своего стиля игры.

sources.citation: Iron Maiden's twin guitar harmonies are the whole foundation of how I approach lead guitar.
sources.author: Synyster Gates

influences.description: М. Шэдоуз называл Guns N' Roses образцом рок-н-ролльной идентичности и мелодического хард-рока.

sources.citation: Guns N' Roses showed us that you could have melody and aggression in the same song. Appetite for Destruction was massive for us.
sources.author: M. Shadows

influences.description: Группа называла Slayer прямым влиянием на агрессивность и скорость своих ранних металкор-записей.

sources.citation: Slayer was a huge influence on our early sound — that speed and aggression on Sounding the Seventh Trumpet.
sources.author: Zacky Vengeance

influences.description: М. Шэдоуз называл Nine Inch Nails влиянием на атмосферные и экспериментальные элементы поздних альбомов.

sources.citation: Nine Inch Nails opened our minds to atmosphere and texture — things you don't normally hear in metal.
sources.author: M. Shadows

influences.description: Synyster Gates называл Тома Морелло и его экспериментальную гитарную технику влиянием на собственный подход к звучанию инструмента.

sources.citation: Tom Morello showed me the guitar could make sounds nobody thought were possible. That experimentation stuck with me.
sources.author: Synyster Gates

influences.description: М. Шэдоуз называл Deftones влиянием на мелодический вокальный подход в тяжёлой музыке.

sources.citation: Deftones showed that you could sing melodically over heavy music and it would still hit just as hard.
sources.author: M. Shadows
